name: Macadamia Chiffon Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour 1/4 cup cold water into a heatproof glass measuring cup. Sprinkle in the gelatin and let soften for 2-3 minutes. Set the cup in a skillet of simmering water and stir the gelatin over low heat until it dissolves. Remove skillet from heat, but leave the cup in to keep the gelatin warm.
2. With a whisk or electric beater, beat the yolks until well blended. Slowly add 1/4 cup of regular sugar and continue beating until the yolks are thick enough to fall in a ribbon when the beater is lifted from the bowl.
3. Pour in boiling water in a thin stream, then pour the mixture into a 1 1/2 to 2 quart enameled or stainless steel saucepan. Stir over low heat until it thickens into a custard heavy enough to coat the spoon. Do not let custard boil, as it may curdle. Remove pan from heat and stir in dissolved gelatin.
4. Strain the custard through a fine sieve set over a deep bowl and add 3 tablespoons of rum and lemon peel. Let custard cool to room temperature, stirring occasionally to prevent it from setting.
5. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites and salt with a clean whisk or beater until frothy. Sprinkle in the remaining regular sugar and continue beating until peaks form. Stir about 1/4 of the whites into the custard, then pour over remaining egg whites and fold together with a spatula.
6. Fold in 1 1/2 cup of chopped nuts. Pour chiffon mixture into pie shell and smooth top with spatula. Refrigerate until serving.
7. Just before serving, beat heavy cream with wire whisk or mixer until thickened. Add superfine sugar and remaining 2 tablespoons rum, continuing to beat until stiff. Spread cream over pie and sprinkle remaining nuts on top.
